title: Player lamp priority and build placement UI chain
summary: Connect the player-facing HUD/action bar to the authoritative lamp/build vertical slice with selection, placement, command, job feedback and failure reasons.
acceptance: Player can select a lamp/build target or build mode, issue a real command through the HUD/action bar, and see authoritative command acceptance or structured rejection
Lamp chain visibly proceeds through job marker, pawn claim, pawn movement, work/progress, completion and alert/resource/status update or blocked reason
Build chain minimally supports selecting build mode, tile hover valid/invalid feedback, blueprint placement, job marker, pawn claim, construction progress and structure completion or blocked reason
Command surfaces are localized in zh-CN and en and never claim unsupported commands as available
Save/load behavior for in-progress or completed command state is explicitly handled or blocked with reviewed reasons
requiredChecks: corepack pnpm exec vitest run --exclude=**/*.e2e.test.ts packages/ui-react/src/shell-hud.test.ts

history.details: Public browser Worker/session bridge decision required: apps/web can send WM-0150 command payloads via @wuming-town/sim-protocol, but it has no reviewed public path to instantiate or talk to the authoritative Simulation Worker. apps/web package boundaries do not allow @wuming-town/sim-worker, and packages/sim-worker/src/browser-worker-entry.ts is internal-only. See coordination/reports/WM-0152.md for options and recommendation.
WM-0157 is done and pushed at 7a24e1e; @wuming-town/sim-worker now exposes the reviewed package-root browser Worker session API for WM-0152 to consume without deep imports or UI authority.

Public authoritative playable projection is still missing after WM-0157. Web can now send WM-0150 commands through @wuming-town/sim-worker, but the reviewed Worker protocol/session surface still exposes only RenderSnapshot/UiDelta hashes and compact summaries, not the full playable read-model needed for HUD, placement, job markers, pawn motion/progress, and blocked reasons. WM-0152 cannot truthfully replace WM-0151 playback until that projection surface exists. See coordination/reports/WM-0152.md for options and recommendation.

WM-0158 is done and pushed at e61aad5; Worker UiDelta now exposes protocol-owned PlayableProjectionV1 via readWebPlayableProjection, so WM-0152 can resume UI wiring without summary parsing or shell-local authority.

WM-0152 remains blocked on a missing public Worker advance surface. apps/web can issue real authoritative WM-0150 lamp/build commands and receive CommandResult acceptance/rejection, but the playable projection stays at tick 0. Public browser-session SetSpeed/Pause do not advance time. The only discovered advance path is an internal Noop command-id convention (wm0150.advance.<tick>) exposed via @wuming-town/sim-core, which task rules forbid importing. Needed decision: expose a legal public advance helper/session API from @wuming-town/sim-worker or @wuming-town/sim-protocol, or make SetSpeed/Pause actually drive browser Worker ticks. Recommended: add a root-level public advance helper/session API; report: coordination/reports/WM-0152.md

WM-0159 is done and pushed at db8a21c; public advance helper advanceWebPlayableWorkerScenarioToTick now lets Web request deterministic authoritative WM-0150 tick progression without sim-core imports or magic command ids.

WM-0152 remains blocked after WM-0159. The legal public advance adapter is integrated and direct local repro reaches authoritative accepted -> moving -> working states for lamp/build, but the required web-shell E2E still times out at 120000ms under the live advance loop. The remaining missing public contract is a reliable authoritative terminal/drain surface for playable command progression: Web/E2E currently must poll projection/debug state while issuing advance requests, and there is no public awaitable UiDelta/terminal/idle helper to stop pumping deterministically. Recommended: add a public sim-worker session helper or reliable message for playable command terminal/drain state. Report: coordination/reports/WM-0152.md

WM-0160 is verified, done, merged and pushed to origin/main at 45f0e5f. Public drain helpers are available via @wuming-town/sim-worker and apps/web/src/simulation-worker-session.ts: waitForWebPlayableProjectionAtOrBeyondTick and drainWebPlayableCommandsToTerminal. WM-0152 can resume without debug polling or sim-core/deep imports.
Implemented authoritative WM-0150 lamp/build HUD chain through public Web Worker projection/drain; required WM-0152 checks pass, including web-shell E2E and pnpm quality.

verified: Verified WM-0152: inspected public Worker projection/drain wiring, player-facing lamp/build command flow, localization/save caveat, and recorded full E2E/quality evidence; local rerun limitations were tooling-only, not assertion failures.
Full integration gates passed on task branch: typecheck, HUD unit, web-shell E2E 13/13, validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, pnpm quality; ready for main merge.

forbiddenPaths: fake shell-local gameplay claimed as authoritative

review.summary: Verified WM-0152: inspected public Worker projection/drain wiring, player-facing lamp/build command flow, localization/save caveat, and recorded full E2E/quality evidence; local rerun limitations were tooling-only, not assertion failures.

integration.summary: Full integration gates passed on task branch: typecheck, HUD unit, web-shell E2E 13/13, validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, pnpm quality; ready for main merge.
